title: Build a health check response
prompt_md:
A health-check endpoint inspects each dependency and reports overall status.

Write `healthcheck(checks)` where `checks` is a dict mapping a dependency name to a boolean (`True` = healthy). Return:

```
{"status": "ok" if every value is True else "degraded",
 "failing": <sorted list of names whose value is False>}
```

The `failing` list must be sorted alphabetically so the output is deterministic.
hints: Collect failing names: [name for name, ok in checks.items() if not ok], then sort it.
status is "ok" when the failing list is empty, otherwise "degraded".
